title: Moeve Supplies Sustainable Aviation Fuel to Condor Flights From Tenerife South to Germany
content: Moeve has completed the supply of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) to Condor for flights departing from Tenerife South Airport to Hamburg, Munich, Frankfurt and DÃ¼sseldorf, highlighting both companiesâ€™ strong commitment to advancing the decarbonization of air travel even though Tenerife South is not yet legally subject to ReFuelEU Aviation requirements. The agreement covered more than 515 metric tons of SAF, produced from used cooking oils and agricultural waste, and is expected to reduce 1,700 tons of lifecycle COâ‚‚ emissions compared with conventional jet fuel. According to airport operator Aena, more than 450,000 passengers a year choose Condor for flights to and from Tenerife South Airport. Ãngel MartÃ­nez Campos, Sustainable Aviation Business Development at Moeve, said this agreement reinforces Moeveâ€™s commitment to lowering emissions in aviation and in an industry as vital to Spain and Tenerife as tourism. Since last year, Condor guests have been able to voluntarily round up their flight booking price or make direct contributions to advance SAF and certified climate protection projects, with funds exclusively allocated to procure and deploy additional SAF beyond regulatory mandates, said Sina Rathgeber, Director Airport Relations, Government Affairs and Sustainability at Condor. The SAF was produced at Moeveâ€™s La RÃ¡bida Energy Park in Huelva, where construction is more than 50% complete on a new secondâ€‘generation biofuels plant with a flexible annual production capacity of 500,000 tons of SAF and renewable diesel, expected to begin production at the end of 2026, making Moeveâ€™s secondâ€‘generation biofuels complex the largest in southern Europe and supporting its Positive Motion strategy to become a European leader in the energy transition by 2030.
author: Analysts Account
